text.tspan: user> Our rate limiter is still the old fixed-window
token bucket in middleware/rateLimiter.ts. Under
bursty traffic it lets through 2x the configured
limit right at window boundaries. Can we refactor
it to a sliding-window log approach?
assistant> Agreed. I'll swap the counter store to a Redis
sorted-set sliding window (ZADD +
ZREMRANGEBYSCORE), keyed per API key. Keeping
the existing token-bucket path alive behind a
feature flag (RATE_LIMIT_STRATEGY=token_bucket
|sliding_window), defaulting to token_bucket
until the new path is proven in staging.
user> Good call on the flag. Ship the sliding window
implementation first, we'll flip the default once
staging looks clean for a day.
assistant> Implemented lib/rateLimiter/slidingWindow.ts
and wired it behind the flag;
middleware/rateLimiter.ts now dispatches on
RATE_LIMIT_STRATEGY. Existing token-bucket unit
tests still pass unmodified. Next: migrate
test/rateLimiter.test.ts to cover both
strategies before flipping the default flag.
# Stop hook: captured 4 turns -> captures/acme-api/raw/
# checkpoint queued for next session start
$ session 2 -- Wednesday, 9:03 AM
[SessionStart] injecting state-of-play for acme-api...
# State of play -- acme-api
## Current focus
Refactoring the rate limiter to a sliding-window log
approach using Redis sorted sets.
## In flight
- Need to migrate the existing rate limiter test suite
in test/rateLimiter.test.ts to cover both the token
bucket and sliding window strategies, as the sliding
window path currently lacks test coverage.
## Recent decisions
- Introduced a feature flag RATE_LIMIT_STRATEGY to
control the rate limiting approach.
- Default setting is token_bucket until the sliding
window implementation is verified in staging.
## Next actions
- Complete test coverage for the sliding-window
implementation in test/rateLimiter.test.ts.
- Monitor staging for a day before switching the
default flag to sliding_window.
